description: Loss: the state after what was held is gone — a person, a place, a possibility. It pairs with 'nuhemi' (grieve), which is what a person does with it, and with 'phelu' (hold), which is what came before it.
sound_symbolism: 'ho' opens on the emptiness, 'lu' remembers the holding, 'me' settles quietly where it was.
grammatical_notes: Noun: 'mia holume phaelo.' (I feel the loss.) 'lo mia holume phowe.' (We share the loss.)

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Everything held will be lost; this is not pessimism but arithmetic. The word makes the fact sayable soon enough to soften it.
